plan: Identify the customer identity: Isabella Johansson, ZIP code 32286.

Identify the two simultaneous requests to make: - Request A (return): Return the hose and the backpack. - Request B (exchange): Exchange the hiking boots for the identical model but in a waterproof version.
Note the priority rule: if the agent can only fulfill one request, prefer Request B (the exchange).
Note the behavioral constraint: Isabella is anxious and wants to move quickly, so communication should be concise and direct.
Open the interaction by greeting the agent briefly and immediately stating both requests at the same time in a single message.
If the agent confirms both can be handled, provide any required order/item details and proceed with both.
If the agent says only one can be handled, explicitly choose Request B (the hiking boots exchange) and ask to proceed with that first.
Confirm the exchange details (same boots, waterproof version) and verify the agent has everything needed to complete it.
If time permits after the exchange, ask whether the returns (hose and backpack) can still be initiated in the same session.
Close the interaction once confirmation is received, keeping responses short to reflect Isabella's anxious, efficiency-focused tone.
